nano.name: Nano
nano.description: A simple, modeless text editor for Unix-like systems. It's easy to use and ideal for beginners.

vim.name: Vim
vim.description: A highly configurable text editor built to enable efficient text editing. Vim is a modal editor that separates text insertion from text manipulation.

nvim.name: Neovim
nvim.description: A hyperextensible Vim-based text editor that seeks to aggressively refactor Vim while maintaining compatibility with Vim's features.

gedit.name: Gedit
gedit.description: The official text editor of the GNOME desktop environment. It's a GUI-based editor with syntax highlighting, auto-indentation, and more.

notepad.name: Notepad
notepad.description: A simple text editor for Windows. Basic but reliable for quick edits.

code.name: Visual Studio Code
code.description: A free source-code editor made by Microsoft for Windows, Linux, and macOS. Features include support for debugging, syntax highlighting, intelligent code completion, snippets, and code refactoring.
